{{Infobox book
| name = Of Darkness and Dawn
| slug = ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn
| precedes = [[Of Kings and Killers (novel)|Of Kings and Killers]]
| follows = [[Of Dawn and Darkness (novel)|Of Dawn and Darkness]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn.jpg|250px]]
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The story begins with a series of violent political upheavals: [[Regent]] [[Alagaeus]] is assassinated by an undercover assassin, and [[Regent]] [[Estyr Six]] survives a firearm attack during a mysterious crop blight. Amidst this instability, [[Shera]], a [[Soulbound]] warrior with a sentient blade named [[Syphren]], undergoes testing under [[High Councilor]] [[Kerian]]. The narrative reveals that ten years prior, [[Shera]] was kidnapped by the [[Council of Architects]] to fulfill a contingency plan: to assassinate [[the Emperor]] before his sanity fails. This mission is complicated by her past as a child assassin, evidenced when she thwarts an attack on [[Regent]] [[Jorin Maze-walker]] by her former peer, [[Benji]].

The tension escalates as the [[Luminian Order]] investigates the disappearance of children and the rising threat of the [[Elders]]. Years ago, during a training session with her teammates [[Lucan]] and [[Meia]], [[the Emperor]] prepared to investigate [[Silverreach]], a town built over an ancient [[library]]. In the present, [[the Emperor]] and his '[[Gardeners]]' discover that [[Silverreach]] has been walled off by the [[Sleepless cult]] and is being manipulated by the Elder [[Ach'magut]]. The Emperor eventually abandons the town, unable to protect it from the stirring Elder.

As political conflicts between [[Imperialists]] and [[Independent Guilds]] intensify, [[Shera]] and her companion [[Ayana]] navigate the [[Luminian]] citadel. Meanwhile, a human slave discovers [[Ach'magut]]'s massive [[library]], where the Elder views him as a catalyst for future change. [[Shera]]’s investigation leads her to a confrontation with the Guild of Magisters{{!}}Magister's Guild; after an ambush by [[Mekendi Maxeus]], [[Shera]] uses her chaotic powers to kill him in a warehouse fire, subsequently clashing with the [[Luminian]] Reader [[Darius Allbright]].

The conflict reaches a breaking point as the [[Elders]] launch a full-scale attack on the Capital. [[Lucan]] is released from prison to destroy the [[Optasia]], [[the Emperor]]'s powerful throne, which has become a conduit for Elder corruption. During a violent confrontation involving the cultist [[Jyrine]], [[Lucan]] is mortally wounded while protecting [[Meia]]. As the [[Imperial]] Palace falls to spider-like [[Inquisitors]], [[the Emperor]] reveals his plan: he must use the [[Optasia]] to combat a global Elder uprising, even if it drives him insane. He commands his [[Gardeners]] to execute him if he loses control.

Following [[Lucan]]'s death, [[Shera]] is consumed by grief on [[Gray Island]]. She experiences a '[[Reader's vision]]' of [[the Emperor]] using his immense power to battle [[Great Elders]] across the globe, culminating in him destroying [[Silverreach]] to strike a blow against the [[Overseer]]. Amidst an impending invasion by the Navigator’s Guild{{!}}Navigator's Guild, [[Shera]] is released from her cell by Yala the High Mason{{!}}High Councilor Yala. Recruiting [[Benji]] and a group of assassins, [[Shera]] prepares to face [[Calder Marten]].

The climax unfolds as [[the Emperor]] successfully uses the [[Optasia]] to defeat [[Ach'magut]] but becomes too dangerous to remain alive. [[Shera]] fulfills her original mission and assassinates him. The Emperor's death triggers a violent release of [[Intent]] and the opening of a supernatural portal, but the [[Gardeners]] escape with the [[Heart of Nakothi]]. In the chaotic aftermath, as [[Guild Heads]] clash and [[Navigator ships]] surround [[Gray Island]], [[Shera]] awakens a new [[Vessel]] to conjure [[Bastion's Veil]]. This act of power leads the surviving [[Consultants]] to recognize [[Shera]] as their new [[Guild Head]].}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Alagaeus]], the [[Regent of the East]], uses his immense earth-shaping powers to brutally crush a group of rebellious soldiers after a peasant woman warns him of an impending insurrection. However, the woman reveals herself to be an assassin, stabbing [[Alagaeus]] in the back and leaving him to die. Meanwhile, [[Estyr Six]], another powerful [[Regent]], investigates a mysterious crop blight and subsequently survives an assassination attempt by a man using an awakened firearm during a house fire.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|To test her new [[Soulbound]] abilities, [[Shera]] is tasked by the [[High Councilor]] [[Kerian]] to execute twelve prisoners in a jungle canyon. After successfully killing eleven of the men using her sentient blade, [[Syphren]], [[Shera]] meets with the [[Regent]] [[Jorin Maze-walker]] to have her weapon stabilized. As they exit his chambers, a gunshot rings out, signaling that their client has been shot.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|Ten years ago, [[Shera]] is kidnapped from her bed on [[Gray Island]] by Shepherds and taken to a specialized chamber designed by [[the Emperor]] to disperse [[Intent]]. There, Yala the High Mason{{!}}High Councilor Yala reveals that the [[Council of Architects]] wants [[Shera]] to fulfill [[the Emperor]]'s own contingency plan: to assassinate him before he is ready. [[Shera]] agrees to the mission, noting that she feels no particular emotion at the prospect of killing him.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|After [[Shera]] thwarts an assassination attempt on [[Jorin]] using a paralytic needle, she discovers the attacker is [[Benji]], a former child assassin from her own past. This event leads to a High [[Council]] meeting where it is revealed that the [[Luminian Order]] has lost many children, prompting a decision to send a [[Gardener]] team to investigate. Meanwhile, [[Shera]] visits the imprisoned [[Lucan]] to share this news, and although he uses his powers to destroy his cell door for an escape, they ultimately decide he must remain imprisoned while she departs on her mission.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Nine]] years ago, [[the Emperor]] conducts a brutal training session with [[Shera]] and her teammates, [[Lucan]] and [[Meia]], on an [[Imperial]] Palace tower. The training is interrupted by a [[Blackwatch]] messenger named [[Grayweather]], who reports that contact has been lost with the city of [[Silverreach]], a location built over an ancient [[library]] where an Elder was once sealed. The Emperor orders [[Shera]] to prepare for combat and meet him at the harbor, intending to investigate the situation personally.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Darius Allbright]], a [[Luminian]] Reader, uses his enchanted [[sword]] to drive an Elder-related Shadow{{!}}shadow away from three young girls who had been acting in eerie unison. After successfully saving the children against the wishes of a skeptical [[Pilgrim]], [[Darius]] is approached by two [[Consultants]], [[Shera]] and [[Ayana]], who reveal that a [[Regent]] has been assassinated. The chapter concludes with the two women abruptly vanishing after discussing their arrival.}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The Emperor and three [[Gardeners]] arrive at the isolated town of [[Silverreach]], where [[Lucan]] discovers the location has been walled off from the outside world. While investigating the town under disguise, the [[Gardeners]] encounter unsettling phenomena and a cult known as the [[Sleepless]], leading [[Shera]] to suspect an Elder is manipulating the inhabitants' minds. The Emperor eventually reveals that the Elder [[Ach'magut]] is stirring and that he can no longer protect the people, prompting him to abandon the town and return to his ship.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Shera]] and [[Ayana]] travel to the [[Luminian]] citadel, [[Hightower]], disguised in poorly fitting clothes to avoid recognition. Upon arrival, they witness a tense standoff between [[Darius Allbright]] and the [[Imperial Guard]], which is resolved without bloodshed. After entering the citadel, they meet with the [[Luminian]] [[Guild Head]], [[Jameson Allbright]], to discuss the escalating political conflict between the [[Imperialists]] and [[Independent Guilds]] before receiving a lead regarding a specific mansion.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|An unnamed human slave escapes the service of the Elder [[Nakothi]] and discovers a massive, ancient [[library]] belonging to a different [[Great Elder]] named [[Ach'magut]]. Upon encountering the entity, the man realizes he is viewed as a psychological anomaly and a potential catalyst for change. [[Ach'magut]] decides to allow the man to remain in the [[library]] to help reveal the future, effectively transitioning him from a slave to a seeker of knowledge.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|While conducting surveillance on a [[Magister]] mansion, [[Shera]] and [[Ayana]] witness a crack appearing in the sky and subsequently capture an assassin named Akko. They are ambushed in a warehouse by [[Mekendi Maxeus]] and a group of [[Magisters]], who reveal they have been aware of the duo's presence all along. Instead of fighting, Mekendi Maxeus{{!}}Maxeus releases them with a letter for their High [[Councilors]], but [[Shera]] impulsively attacks and drains the power from a [[Magister]] before fleeing into the shadows.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Mekendi Maxeus]], Head of the Guild of Magisters{{!}}Magister's Guild, attempts to frame two assassins for an attack, but his plan unravels when the [[Soulbound]] woman, [[Shera]], uses her chaotic powers to fight back. Amidst a warehouse fire and the arrival of the [[Luminian Order]], [[Shera]] manages to kill Mekendi Maxeus{{!}}Maxeus by piercing his defenses with her [[Vessel]]. The chapter concludes with [[Shera]] engaging in a brief, unstable skirmish with [[Darius Allbright]] before collapsing from exhaustion.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Eight]] years in the past, [[Shera]] meets with Yala the High Mason{{!}}High Councilor Yala and later confronts [[Meia]], who has been secretly following her. The two argue over [[the Emperor]]'s stability and whether he should be assassinated, highlighting a rift between [[Meia]]'s loyalty to The Empire{{!}}the Empire and [[Shera]]'s distrust of [[the Emperor]]'s sanity. The chapter concludes with [[Lucan]] joining them, as the three [[Gardeners]] agree to act as a team and keep one another informed before taking future actions.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|After a crack appears in the sky, [[Lucan]] is released from his cell by Yala the High Mason{{!}}High Councilor Yala on a mission to secure or destroy the [[Optasia]], [[the Emperor]]'s powerful throne, which has been targeted by the [[Sleepless]]. [[Lucan]] infiltrates the [[Imperial]] Palace and discovers the device is surrounded by grotesque, flesh-like Elder corruption. Upon encountering [[Calder Marten]] and his crew outside the Emperor's personal rooms{{!}}Emperor's chambers, [[Lucan]] uses his unique [[Reading]] abilities to manipulate the palace architecture and engage them in combat.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|During a training session five years ago, [[General Jarelys Teach]] takes [[Shera]] to a private bathhouse to reveal that [[the Emperor]] has tasked her with helping [[Shera]] assassinate him. [[Teach]] shares her history of rising through the [[Imperial Guard]] and promises to stand against [[the Emperor]] herself if he loses his sanity, while also providing [[Shera]] with technical advice on how to destroy the [[Heart of Nakothi]]. The conversation is abruptly interrupted when a guard arrives to report that the Capital is under attack by the [[Elders]].}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|After subduing the Navigators, [[Lucan]] warns [[Calder Marten]] that the [[Emperor's Optasia]] is a dangerous conduit to the [[Great Elders]] that must be destroyed. The tension escalates when [[Calder]] introduces [[Meia]] and his wife, [[Jyrine]], a dedicated Elder cultists{{!}}Elder cultist, to debate the device's use. The confrontation turns violent when [[Jyrine]] attacks [[Meia]] with a [[green flame]], prompting [[Lucan]] to protect [[Meia]] with his [[Intent]] before being mortally wounded by [[Jyrine]] herself.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|As the [[Imperial]] Palace is overrun by spider-like [[Inquisitors]], [[Shera]] reunites with [[Lucan]] and [[Meia]] to discover that the [[Elders]] are conducting experiments on humans and attempting to corrupt [[the Emperor]]. The Emperor reveals that he must use the [[Optasia]], a global divination device, to combat a worldwide uprising of [[Elders]], despite the risk that connecting to it will drive him insane. He commands the [[Gardeners]] to execute him if he loses control of himself during the process.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|After a two-day journey to the [[Gray Island]], [[Shera]] is met by [[High Councilor]] [[Tyril]] and [[Darius]], while she is escorted to meet [[High Councilor]] [[Kerian]]. [[Kerian]] informs [[Shera]] that her recent actions have likely triggered a guild war and reveals the devastating news that [[Lucan]] has died following his successful mission to destroy the [[Optasia]]. [[Shera]] is confined to [[Lucan]]'s former cell, where she eventually faces his body and struggles to process her grief.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|While caught in [[the Emperor]]'s "[[Reader's vision]]," [[Shera]] witnesses a series of rapid, global events where [[the Emperor]] uses his immense power to combat various [[Great Elders]] and their monstrous spawn. The vision transitions from jungle battles and underwater encounters with massive creatures to a confrontation with the [[Great Elder]] [[Ach'magut]] in a vast underground [[library]]. To conclude the vision, [[the Emperor]] uses his [[Intent]] to completely destroy the town of [[Silverreach]], collapsing it into the [[library]] to strike a final blow against the [[Overseer]].}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|As the Navigator’s Guild{{!}}Navigator's Guild prepares to attack [[Gray Island]], [[Shera]] is released from her cell by [[Yala]], who admits to sending [[Lucan]] to his death. [[Shera]] retrieves her [[Vessel]], [[Syphren]], and recruits a group of trained assassins led by [[Benji]] to assist in her mission to kill [[Calder Marten]]. The chapter concludes as the enemy arrives early, prompting [[Shera]] to head into the battle's mist to hunt her targets.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|After [[the Emperor]] successfully uses the [[Optasia]] to defeat the Elder [[Ach'magut]], [[Shera]] realizes he has become too dangerous to live and assassinates him while he is weakened. The Emperor's death triggers a violent release of [[Intent]] and a supernatural portal, but the [[Gardeners]] manage to escape with the [[Heart of Nakothi]]. Following the mission, [[Shera]] and [[Lucan]] reflect on the High [[Council]]'s suspicious motives for ordering the assassination and prepare to investigate further.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|As the [[Navigator ships]] approach, [[Kerian]] uses a glass box to lure [[Guild Heads]] [[Bliss]] and [[Jarelys Teach]] into a confrontation that results in a destructive magical clash. During the chaos, [[Bliss]] uses her bone spear to destroy the glass box and weaken [[Bastion's Veil]], forcing the [[Consultants]] to retreat. In the aftermath, [[Shera]] attempts to salvage the remaining mist with her weapon and flees into the woods with [[Meia]] as [[Calder]] approaches.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|Twenty-two years ago, [[High Mason]] [[Yala]] spared [[Rudeus Maxwell]] from execution by assigning him a secret mission to build an army for the [[Consultants]]. In the present day, [[Estyr Six]] finds [[Jorin Curse-breaker]] on a blighted [[Gray Island]] and learns that the Champion’s Guild{{!}}Champion's Guild has joined [[Captain Marten]] to oppose the new Emperor. As the remnants of the [[Consultants]] are surrounded by [[Navigator ships]], [[Shera]] uses a newly awakened [[Vessel]] to conjure [[Bastion's Veil]], leading the surviving [[Consultants]] to recognize her as their new [[Guild Head]].}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Emperor (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Shera]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Lucan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Meia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Elderspawn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Maxwell]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Ayana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Reader (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Dead Mother]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Yala]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Darius Allbright]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Jorin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Kerian]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Alagaeus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Darius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Calder Marten]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Urg’naut]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Kelarac]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Mekendi Maxeus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Hansin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Loreli]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Bliss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Jorin Curse-breaker]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Pilgrim]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Regent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Andel Petronus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Kthanikahr]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Guild Head]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Jarelys Teach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Zhen]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gray Island]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gardeners]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Imperial Guard]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Optasia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Consultants]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Luminian]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Silverreach]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Blackwatch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Bastion's Veil]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Great Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Readers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Council of Architects]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Shepherds (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Gardener]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Consultant's Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Estyr Six]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Empire (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Luminian Order]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Regents]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Architects]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Luminian Knights]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Imperial Palace (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Aion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Champions]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Masons]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[High Council (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Aion Sea]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Magisters]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Greenwardens]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Sleepless]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Consultant]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Aurelian Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Heartland]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Luminian cathedral]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Witnesses (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Capital (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Navigators (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[High Councilors (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Reading]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Reader senses]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Syphren]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Heart of Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Tyrfang]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[The Testament]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Soulbound Vessel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Spear of Tharlos]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Intent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Ach'magut]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Soulbound]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Great Elder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Kameira]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Othaghor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Vessel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Vessels]]}}

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
